messages.content: You are the planning module of a patent-drafting agent. Given an invention disclosure as JSON, reply with a short JSON plan of drafting steps. Reply with JSON only.

{"id":"uspto-19387326","title":"UE CAPABILITY REPORTING AND HANDLING OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE/MACHINE LEARNING MODELS","inventors":["Thomas WIRTH","Cornelius Hellge","Tatiana RYKOV","Thomas Fehrenbach","Baris Göktepe","Thomas Schierl","Thomas Wiegand"],"technology_class":"G06N","summary":"A user device, UE, for a wireless communication network, like a 3rd Generation Partnership Project, 3GPP, network, is described. The UE has a plurality of UE capabilities, the UE capabilities indicating functions the UE is capable to perform. The UE is to inform on one or more parts of the plurality of UE capabilities using one or more identification values, e.g., hash values.","details":"Please amend the claims as follows:
1
1. (Currently Amended) A user device, UE
, for a wireless communication network, like a 3rd Generation Partnership Project, 3GPP, network,
wherein the UE
 comprises a plurality of UE
 capabilities, the UE
 capabilities indicating functions the UE
 is capable to perform, and
wherein the UE
 is to inform on one or more parts of the plurality of UE
 capabilities using one or more identification values, e.g., hash values,

wherein the UE
 is capable to use one or more artificial intellig
ence / machine learning, AI/
ML, models, and the plurality of UE
 capabilities comprises information on the one or more AI/
ML models,
wherein the UE
 is to store additional information for identifying and/or executing a
 certain AI/
ML model, the additional information including a validity of the certain AI/
ML model and a cell ID linked to the certain AI/
ML model, and
wherein the validity of an AI/
ML model is associated with a sig
naling
 of AI/
ML model update or capability information, and the validity of the certain AI/
ML model is determined when the UE
 is associated with the cell ID, and/or the certain AI/ML model is valid when the UE
 is associated with the cell ID.
Currently amended

2
2. (Canceled).
Canceled

3
3. (Currently Amended) The user device, UE, of claim [[2]] 1,
 wherein:

[[-]] each of the one or more AI/
ML models is identified by an individual identification value, or [[
-]] two or more of the AI/
ML models are identified by a common identification value.
Currently amended

4
4. (Currently Amended) The user device, UE, of claim [[2]] 1, wherein:

[[-]] the UE
 is to receive a request, like a UE
 capability enquiry message, from the wireless communication network or from a further UE
 to report some or all of the AI/
ML models supported by the UE, and/or [[-]] responsive to the request, the UE
 is to send the one or more identification values representing the supported AI/
ML models to the wireless communication network or to the further UE.

Currently amended

5
5. (Currently Amended) The user device, UE, of claim 4, 
wherein:

[[
-]] the UE
 is to receive from the wireless communication network or from a further UE
 a request indicating one or more identification values or a common identification value representing AI/
ML models, and/or [[
-]] responsive to the request, the UE
 is to:
[[o]] deactivate the one or more AI/
ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value, or [[o]] reset the one or more AI
/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value into a predefined state.

Currently amended

6
6. (Currently Amended) The user device, UE
, of claim [[2]] 1,
 wherein:

[[-]] the UE
 is to receive from the wireless communication network or from a further UE
 one or more additional AI/
ML models to be supported by the UE
, and/or [[
-]] store the one or more additional AI/
ML models.

2]] 1,
 wherein:

[[
-]] the UE
 is to receive from the wireless communication network or from a further UE

ML models, and/or [[
-]] responsive to the message, the UE
 is to:
[
[o]] use the one or more AI/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value as default AI/
ML model, or

[[o]] use among the one or more AI/
ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value a first AI/
ML model supported by the UE
 as default AI/
ML model, or [[o]] bar the one or more AI/
ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value from being used by the UE.

ML model.

, of claim 8,

ML model is identified using the identification value of the AI/
ML model and the hierarchy level at which the AI/
ML model is stored.
Currently amended

transcript.reply:
{
  "drafting_plan": [
    "1. Review the current claim set and identify all dependent claims that reference claim 2 (the base claim).",
    "2. Extract the core technical features related to AI/ML model identification, validity, storage, and signaling from the detailed description.",
    "3. Draft independent claim 1 that captures the broad inventive concept of a UE reporting AI/ML model capabilities using identification values (e.g., hash values).",
    "4. Draft dependent claim 2 that adds the capability to store additional information (validity and cell ID) for each AI/ML model.",
    "5. Draft dependent claim 3 that defines how identification values are assigned (individual vs. common) for AI/ML models.",
    "6. Draft dependent claim 4 that describes receiving a capability enquiry and reporting identification values to the network or another UE.",
    "7. Draft dependent claim 5 that details actions taken upon receiving a request (deactivation, resetting to a predefined state).",
    "8. Draft dependent claim 6 that covers receiving a default AI/ML model indication via MIB/SIB and using it as the default or barring it.",
    "9. Draft dependent claim 7 that defines barring information including identification values, barring time, and trigger conditions.",
    "10. Draft dependent claim 8 that adds signaling of barring information to the network or another UE.",
    "11. Draft dependent claim 9 that links AI/ML models to their origin (e.g., server or network) when using non‑unique identification values.",
    "12. Draft dependent claim 10 that describes re‑activating a model after its last use using the stored identification value.",
    "13. Draft dependent claim 11 that outlines updating AI/ML models (partial/full updates, pull/push, validity condition changes).",
    "14. Draft dependent claim 12 that specifies hierarchical storage of models and identification via model hash and hierarchy level.",
    "15. Draft dependent claim 13 that defines a method for a network entity to receive UE capability information and determine capabilities from identification values.",
    "16. Ensure each claim includes all necessary structural and functional elements as disclosed in the detailed description.",
    "17. Perform a consistency check to confirm that claim dependencies and terminology match the amendment language.",
    "18. Prepare the final claim set in proper USPTO claim formatting (numbered, with appropriate punctuation and capitalization)."
  ]
}

messages.content:
You are a patent attorney. Draft numbered patent claims (independent claim 1, then dependent claims) followed by a specification section for the disclosed invention. Close common loophole patterns: antecedent-basis gaps, means-plus-function overbreadth, missing dependent-claim fallbacks.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

Loophole guardrails retrieved from memory for this technology class — close each one with explicit claim language; if none are listed, draft with standard care:
(none on record)

    "3. Draft independent claim 1 that captures the broad inventive concept of a UE reporting AI/ML model capabilities using identification values (e.g., hash values).",
    "4. Draft dependent claim 2 that adds the capability to store additional information (validity and cell ID) for each AI/ML model.",
    "5. Draft dependent claim 3 that defines how identification values are assigned (individual vs. common) for AI/ML models.",
    "6. Draft dependent claim 4 that describes receiving a capability enquiry and reporting identification values to the network or another UE.",
    "7. Draft dependent claim 5 that details actions taken upon receiving a request (deactivation, resetting to a predefined state).",
    "8. Draft dependent claim 6 that covers receiving a default AI/ML model indication via MIB/SIB and using it as the default or barring it.",
    "9. Draft dependent claim 7 that defines barring information including identification values, barring time, and trigger conditions.",
    "10. Draft dependent claim 8 that adds signaling of barring information to the network or another UE.",
    "11. Draft dependent claim 9 that links AI/ML models to their origin (e.g., server or network) when using non‑unique identification values.",
    "12. Draft dependent claim 10 that describes re‑activating a model after its last use using the stored identification value.",
    "13. Draft dependent claim 11 that outlines updating AI/ML models (partial/full updates, pull/push, validity condition changes).",
    "14. Draft dependent claim 12 that specifies hierarchical storage of models and identification via model hash and hierarchy level.",
    "15. Draft dependent claim 13 that defines a method for a network entity to receive UE capability information and determine capabilities from identification values.",
    "16. Ensure each claim includes all necessary structural and functional elements as disclosed in the detailed description.",
    "17. Perform a consistency check to confirm that claim dependencies and terminology match the amendment language.",
    "18. Prepare the final claim set in proper USPTO claim formatting (numbered, with appropriate punctuation and capitalization)."
  ]
}

transcript.reply:
**1.** A user device, UE, for a wireless communication network, comprising:  
a plurality of UE capabilities indicating functions the UE is capable to perform;  
wherein the UE is to report one or more parts of the plurality of UE capabilities using one or more identification values, e.g., hash values;  
wherein the UE is capable of using one or more artificial intelligence/machine learning, AI/ML, models;  
wherein the plurality of UE capabilities comprises information on the one or more AI/ML models;  
wherein the UE is to store additional information for identifying and/or executing a certain AI/ML model, the additional information including a validity of the certain AI/ML model and a cell ID linked to the certain AI/ML model;  
wherein the validity of an AI/ML model is associated with signaling of AI/ML model update or capability information, and the validity of the certain AI/ML model is determined when the UE is associated with the cell ID, and/or the certain AI/ML model is valid when the UE is associated with the cell ID.

**2.** The UE of claim 1, wherein each of the one or more AI/ML models is identified by an individual identification value, or two or more of the AI/ML models are identified by a common identification value.

**3.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE is to receive a request, like a UE capability enquiry message, from the wireless communication network or from a further UE to report some or all of the AI/ML models supported by the UE, and/or responsive to the request, the UE is to send the one or more identification values representing the supported AI/ML models to the wireless communication network or to the further UE.

**4.** The UE of claim 3, wherein the UE is to receive from the wireless communication network or from a further UE a request indicating one or more identification values or a common identification value representing AI/ML models, and/or responsive to the request, the UE is to: deactivate the one or more AI/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value, or reset the one or more AI/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value into a predefined state.

**5.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE is to receive from the wireless communication network or from a further UE one or more additional AI/ML models to be supported by the UE, and/or store the one or more additional AI/ML models.

**6.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE is to receive from the wireless communication network or from a further UE a message, like a Master Information Block, MIB, or a System Information Block, SIB, indicating one or more identification values or a common identification value representing AI/ML models, and/or responsive to the message, the UE is to: use the one or more AI/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value as default AI/ML model, or use among the one or more AI/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value a first AI/ML model supported by the UE as default AI/ML model, or bar the one or more AI/ML models indicated by the one or more identification values or the common identification value from being used by the UE.

**7.** The UE of claim 6, wherein the UE includes within barring information one or more: the one or more identification values of the one or more AI/ML models it has barred, a barring time, e.g., how long the model is barred, a trigger condition that has to be fulfilled to unbar the AI/ML model.

**8.** The UE of claim 8, wherein the UE is to signal the barring information to the wireless communication network or to a further UE.

**9.** The UE of claim 1, wherein an AI/ML model is identified by a non‑unique identification value used at different origins of the AI/ML model, like different AI/ML servers or different wireless communication networks, and wherein the UE is to link the AI/ML model with the origin of the AI/ML model.

**10.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE is to associate each of the stored AI/ML models with an identification value, like a hash value, and wherein the UE is to re‑activate an AI/ML model after the last use using the identification value.

**11.** The UE of claim 1, wherein an AI/ML model is updated partly or in full, and/or the UE is to pull an AI/ML model update, and/or an AI/ML model update is pushed to the UE, and/or an AI/ML model is updated in case a difference is detected between the AI/ML model stored at the UE and the AI/ML model stored at an origin of the AI/ML model, and/or an AI/ML model update comprises an update of one or more validity conditions of the AI/ML model, e.g., a change in the validity time, or a change of a geographical area in which the UE is located, or a use of a different set of resources, like a different resource pool.

messages.content:
You are a hostile patent examiner. Attack the draft below: find §112 indefiniteness, antecedent-basis errors, §101/§102/§103 exposure, and claim language a competitor could design around. Ground every defect in the standards below and cite the MPEP section. Reply as a bulleted list of defects, most severe first.

PATENT-LAW STANDARDS (US MPEP) — apply these exactly; cite the section when you raise or fix a defect:
- §101 subject-matter eligibility (Alice/Mayo two-step): a claim directed to an abstract idea (Step 2A Prong 1) that is not integrated into a practical application (Prong 2) and adds no inventive concept beyond well-understood, routine, conventional activity (Step 2B) is ineligible. Software fix: recite a specific technical improvement to computer functionality, not the abstract idea run on a generic computer. [MPEP 2106]
- §102 anticipation: a single prior-art reference disclosing — expressly or inherently — every claim limitation arranged as in the claim anticipates it. Fix: add a limitation no single reference discloses. [MPEP 2131]
- §103 obviousness (Graham / KSR): weigh the scope and content of the prior art, the differences from the claim, and the level of ordinary skill; a combination of known elements yielding predictable results is obvious absent a limitation with an unexpected result or no motivation to combine. Fix: claim a non-obvious combination and record unexpected results or other secondary considerations. [MPEP 2141-2143]
- §112(a) written description & enablement: the specification must show possession of the full claimed scope and enable a skilled artisan to make and use it without undue experimentation (Wands factors). Fix: support every claim term and the full claimed range in the specification. [MPEP 2163-2164]
- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. Fix: add antecedent basis and a definite standard for any relative term. [MPEP 2173]
- §112(f) means-plus-function: 'means for [function]' — or a nonce word ('module', 'mechanism', 'unit' for [function]) without recited structure — invokes §112(f) and is indefinite unless the specification discloses the corresponding structure (for a computer function, the specific algorithm). Fix: recite definite structure, or disclose the performing algorithm in the specification. [MPEP 2181]

transcript.reply:
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – “one or more identification values” and “a common identification value” lack antecedent basis and a definite standard** – the claim recites “one or more identification values” and “a common identification value” without defining what those values are, how they are formed, or what they identify; the specification never links a specific hash or other identifier to a particular AI/ML model, violating the “reasonable certainty” requirement of Nautilus and MPEP 2173.  
- **§112(a) written‑description & enablement – failure to disclose the full scope of “identification values” and “validity of the certain AI/ML model”** – the specification only describes storing “additional information” that includes validity and a cell ID, but it does not enable a person of ordinary skill to determine the full range of possible identification values, their formats, or how to generate them; thus the claim is not adequately supported (MPEP 2163‑2164).  
- **§101 subject‑matter eligibility – abstract‑idea risk** – the claim is directed to a generic “UE” that merely reports “identification values” and “validity” of AI/ML models; it does not recite a specific technical improvement to computer operation (e.g., a novel model‑update mechanism, resource‑allocation method, or AI‑driven power‑control scheme) and therefore is an abstract idea applied on a generic device (MPEP 2106).  
- **§102 anticipation – prior‑art disclosure of the same functional language** – a single prior‑art reference (e.g., 3GPP TS 38.331 describing UE capability reporting and AI/ML model identification) discloses each of the claim’s limitations: reporting identification values, linking validity to a cell ID, receiving requests to deactivate or reset models, and using identification values as default models; thus the claim is anticipated (MPEP 2131).  
- **§103 obviousness – predictable combination of known elements** – the claimed features (storing AI/ML model validity tied to a cell ID, reporting identification values, receiving a request to bar models, and using a common identification value) are all known in the art; combining them yields a predictable result of managing AI/ML model usage, with no unexpected technical effect shown, making the invention obvious (MPEP 2141‑2143).  
- **§112(f) means‑plus‑function exposure – “identification value” and “validity” used as functional language** – the claim recites “identification value” and “validity … is determined when the UE is associated with the cell ID” as functional limitations without providing the corresponding structure (e.g., specific hash algorithm, algorithmic steps, or structural means); this triggers §112(f) indefiniteness unless the specification discloses the exact algorithmic structure, which it does not (MPEP 2181).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – relative terms “one or more”, “certain”, “predefined state”, “different resource pool” lack benchmarks** – the claim uses vague quantifiers and relative conditions without defining what constitutes “one or more”, what a “predefined state” is, or how a “different resource pool” is identified; these terms are not given a definite meaning, violating the certainty requirement (MPEP 2173).  
- **§102/§103 prior‑art anticipation of “barred AI/ML model” signaling** – the concept of barring an AI/ML model based on a cell‑ID‑linked validity and signaling barring information is disclosed in standard‑essential documents (e.g., 3GPP TS 38.331 and TS 38.321) that describe UE capability reporting and model activation/deactivation; therefore the claim’s “bar” limitation is anticipated and obvious (MPEP 2131, 2141‑2143).  
- **§112(a) enablement – no teaching of how to generate or validate the “identification values” or how to update the AI/ML model validity conditions** – the specification does not enable a skilled artisan to practice the claimed method of pulling or pushing model updates, detecting differences, or changing validity time/area; thus the full claimed scope is not enabled (MPEP 2163‑2164).  
- **§112(b) indefiniteness – “common identification value” lacks structural antecedent** – the claim refers to “a common identification value” without specifying which model or which set of models share it, leaving the scope undefined; the specification never ties a common value to a particular group of models, rendering the claim indefinite (MPEP 2173).  
- **§101 subject‑matter eligibility – lack of concrete technical implementation of AI/ML model update** – the claim merely states that an AI/ML model may be updated “partly

- §112(b) definiteness (Nautilus 'reasonable certainty'): the claims must define scope with reasonable certainty; terms of degree ('substantially', 'about') need a benchmark, and every 'the'/'said' element needs antecedent basis. transcript.reply:
**1.** A user equipment (UE) for a wireless communication network, comprising:  
a capability list storing a plurality of capability identifiers, each capability identifier corresponding to a specific AI/ML model supported by the UE;  
wherein the UE determines a validity status for each AI/ML model by evaluating a validity condition that includes at least one of (i) a model‑update signaling indicator received from a serving cell identified by a cell identifier, and (ii) a time‑based validity window associated with the serving cell;  
wherein the UE generates, for each supported AI/ML model, a hash‑based identification value that is derived from the model’s binary representation and a secret key;  
wherein the UE transmits, to a network entity, a capability report that includes one or more of the generated hash‑based identification values together with the associated validity status;  
wherein the UE receives, from the network entity, a deactivation request that specifies one or more hash‑based identification values; and  
wherein, in response to the deactivation request, the UE places the AI/ML models identified by the specified hash‑based identification values into a disabled state and records a barring information element that indicates a barring duration and a trigger condition for re‑enabling the models.

**2.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the capability list may store multiple hash‑based identification values for a single AI/ML model, and wherein the UE may transmit a common hash‑based identification value that represents a group of AI/ML models sharing an identical validity condition.

**3.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE may receive, from the network entity or from a secondary UE, a capability‑enquiry message that requests the UE to report some or all of the hash‑based identification values of the supported AI/ML models, and wherein the UE may respond by transmitting the requested hash‑based identification values together with their current validity status.

**4.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE may receive, from the network entity or from a secondary UE, a request that includes one or more hash‑based identification values or a common hash‑based identification value, and wherein, in response, the UE may deactivate the AI/ML models indicated by the specified hash‑based identification values or may reset the indicated models to a predefined idle state.

**5.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE may receive, from the network entity or from a secondary UE, one or more additional hash‑based identification values corresponding to further AI/ML models to be supported, and wherein the UE may store the additional hash‑based identification values in the capability list.

**6.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE may receive, from the network entity or from a secondary UE, a system‑information block (SIB) that includes one or more hash‑based identification values or a common hash‑based identification value representing AI/ML models designated as default, and wherein the UE may select one of the indicated models as the default AI/ML model for operation.

**7.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the barring information element includes at least one of: (i) the hash‑based identification value(s) of the barred AI/ML models, (ii) a barring duration indicating how long the model remains barred, and (iii) a trigger condition that must be satisfied before the barred model may be unbarred.

**8.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE may transmit the barring information element to the network entity or to a secondary UE to indicate which AI/ML models are currently barred and under what conditions they may be re‑activated.

**9.** The UE of claim 1, wherein a hash‑based identification value may be non‑unique across different origins of the AI/ML model, such as different AI/ML servers or different wireless communication networks, and wherein the UE may associate each stored AI/ML model with the origin information that generated its hash‑based identification value.

**10.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the UE may re‑activate an AI/ML model after its last use by referencing the hash‑based identification value associated with that model and by evaluating the current validity condition before use.

**11.** The UE of claim 1, wherein an AI/ML model may be updated in whole or in part, and wherein the UE may pull an AI/ML model update from a network server or may receive a pushed AI/ML model update, and wherein the UE may detect a difference between the stored model and the updated model and may adjust the validity condition accordingly, including changes to the validity time, geographical area, or resource‑pool allocation.

**12.** The UE of claim 1, wherein the validity condition further includes a geographical‑area indicator that is derived from the serving cell identifier, and wherein the UE may modify the validity status of an AI/ML model when the UE moves out of the indicated geographical area.

---

**Specification (excerpt, same format as original)**  

The present disclosure relates to wireless communication systems, and more particularly to techniques for managing artificial‑intelligence/machine‑learning (AI/ML) models within a user equipment (UE) in a cellular network.  

In modern 5G‑Advanced and beyond networks, UEs are increasingly equipped

wherein the UE determines a validity status for each AI/ML model by evaluating a validity condition that includes at least one of (i) a model‑update signaling indicator received from a serving cell identified by a cell identifier, and (ii) a time‑based validity window associated with the serving cell; wherein the UE generates, for each supported AI/ML model, a hash‑based identification value that is derived from the model’s binary representation and a secret key; wherein the UE transmits, to a network entity, a capability report that includes one or more of the generated hash‑based identification values together with the associated validity status; wherein the UE receives, from the network entity, a deactivation request that specifies one or more hash‑based identification values; and wherein, in response to the deactivation request, the UE places the AI/ML models identified by the specified hash‑based identification values into a disabled state and records a barring information element that indicates a barring duration and a trigger condition for re‑enabling the models.

defects.quote: a capability list storing a plurality of capability identifiers, each capability identifier corresponding to a specific AI/ML model supported by the UE
